raw_consumer_statement:
I disputed an {$18000.00} charge from XXXX on my credit card following the sudden death of my father. In my grief, I misread the one-way fare as {$1800.00} instead of {$18000.00}. I would never have knowingly agreed to this amount. 

Both XXXX and XXXX acknowledged my situation, requested my fathers death certificate, and recognized the exceptional circumstances. I provided all required documentation. 

My issuing bank, AMEX, initially credited the full amount after a couple of days, acknowledging the circumstances. However, they later rebilled the charge despite the humanitarian situation, relying solely on the merchants claim that the service was delivered. This dispute is not about service delivery. 

I request CFPB intervention because my bank did not protect me as a customer in this clear, exceptional, and humanitarian case. If the merchant refuses or fails to refund, I expect my issuing bank, AMEX, to assist me or provide a refund for this honest mistake, which is a huge financial burden during this difficult time of loss of my father. 

I have all screenshots and documentation of communications with the merchants and my bank, which I am prepared to provide.
